title: Add language-specific linting and formatting for Python and Java bots
body:
The repo's mixed Python/Java/JS codebase caused ESLint and Prettier to run against non-JS files, producing spurious failures. This adds per-language lint/format checks with proper scoping.

## New workflow: `language-specific-lint.yml`

Four-job pipeline triggered on push/PR to `main`/`master`:

- **`detect-languages`** — diffs changed files, outputs `has_python` / `has_java` / `has_js` flags to gate downstream jobs
- **`lint-python`** — Flake8 (120-char limit) + Black format check, scoped to `python_bots/`
- **`lint-java`** — Checkstyle + google-java-format, scoped to `java_bots/`; JARs cached between runs
- **`lint-js`** — ESLint + Prettier restricted to `.js/.ts/.jsx/.tsx` only; skipped when no JS/TS files changed

Each job runs only when its language category has changed files, and each carries `permissions: contents: read`.

## Supporting files

- **`checkstyle.xml`** — Google Java Style–based rules (naming, braces, imports, whitespace)
- **`python_bots/README.md`** / **`java_bots/README.md`** — directory conventions and local tool usage
- **`.prettierignore`** — adds `*.java`, `python_bots/`, `java_bots/` to prevent Prettier from touching non-JS files

title: Update CodeQL workflow to Java with current action versions and PR-open triggers
body:
This updates the CodeQL workflow to analyze Java and align with current GitHub Action major versions. It also ensures scans run on pushes to `main` and when PRs targeting `main` are opened (and updated).

- **Workflow trigger behavior**
  - Keep `push` on `main`
  - Set `pull_request` on `main` with explicit event types:
    - `opened` (new PRs)
    - `synchronize` (new commits on existing PRs)

- **CodeQL runtime/update**
  - Upgrade workflow actions to current majors:
    - `actions/checkout@v4`
    - `github/codeql-action/init@v3`
    - `github/codeql-action/autobuild@v3`
    - `github/codeql-action/analyze@v3`

- **Language target**
  - Change CodeQL matrix language from `python` to `java`

```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    types: [opened, synchronize]

strategy:
  matrix:
    language: ["java"]
```

body:
Converts `hello.py` to idiomatic Java as required, placing the output in `java_translation/Hello.java`.

## Changes

- **`java_translation/Hello.java`** — New file. Translates three functions and the main entry point:
  - `greet(String name)` → string concatenation
  - `add(int a, int b)` → integer addition
  - `fibonacci(int n)` → iterative `List<Integer>` build, matching Python semantics (empty list for `n≤0`, `[0]` for `n=1`)

```java
public static List<Integer> fibonacci(int n) {
    if (n <= 0) return new ArrayList<>();
    if (n == 1) { ... return List([0]); }
    List<Integer> fib = new ArrayList<>(List.of(0, 1));
    for (int i = 2; i < n; i++)
        fib.add(fib.get(i - 1) + fib.get(i - 2));
    return fib;
}
```

Original `hello.py` is untouched.

title: Replace Python client with Java Spring Boot ONVIF Profile T SOAP 1.2 client
body:
Reimplemented ONVIF client in Java Spring Boot with Apache CXF for SOAP 1.2 protocol support, designed to work with hediyenurtut/OnvifServer.

## Architecture

- **Stack**: Java 17, Spring Boot 3.2.3, Apache CXF 4.0.3, JAXB for XML binding
- **Protocol**: SOAP 1.2 (`application/soap+xml`, envelope namespace `http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope`)
- **Services**: Device Management, Network Configuration, PTZ Control, Media Operations, Imaging Settings
- **REST Facade**: HTTP endpoints at `/api/onvif/*` for all ONVIF operations

## Implementation

**Models** (52 classes)
- JAXB-annotated request/response models for all operations
- Packages: `device`, `network`, `ptz`, `media`, `imaging`
- Support for H.264/H.265 encoder configurations

**Services** (5 classes)
- `OnvifDeviceService`: GetDeviceInformation, GetServices, GetHostname, SetHostname
- `OnvifNetworkService`: Get/SetNetworkInterfaces, Get/SetNetworkDefaultGateway  
- `OnvifPTZService`: Move, ContinuousMove, Stop, Presets, HomePosition, GetMoveOptions
- `OnvifMediaService`: GetVideoSources, GetVideoEncoderConfigurations, AddConfiguration
- `OnvifImagingService`: Get/SetImageSettings, GetOptions

**Configuration**
```properties
onvif.camera.host=192.168.1.100
onvif.camera.port=8080
onvif.camera.service-path=/onvif/device_service
```

## Example Usage

```java
// Service layer (SOAP)
@Autowired
private OnvifPTZService ptzService;

ptzService.continuousMove(profileToken, 
    new PTZVector(new Vector2D(0.5f, 0.0f), new Vector1D(0.0f)));
ptzService.stop(profileToken, true, true);

// REST API
POST /api/onvif/ptz/continuous-move?profileToken=profile1
{
  "panTilt": {"x": 0.5, "y": 0.0},
  "zoom": {"x": 0.0}
}
```

## SOAP Request Structure

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soap:Envelope 
  xmlns:soap="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope"
  xmlns:tptz="http://www.onvif.org/ver20/ptz/wsdl"
  xmlns:tt="http://www.onvif.org/ver10/schema">
  <soap:Body>
    <tptz:ContinuousMove>
      <tptz:ProfileToken>profile1</tptz:ProfileToken>
      <tptz:Velocity>...</tptz:Velocity>
    </tptz:ContinuousMove>
  </soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>
```

All requests use `Content-Type: application/soap+xml; charset=utf-8` per ONVIF Profile T specification.

title: Convert Python project to Java Spring Boot with optimized CSV writing
body:
Converts the NBA odds fetcher from Python to Java 17 / Spring Boot 3.2.5 and optimizes CSV generation based on benchmarking.

## Migration

- **Stack**: Python 3.12 + `requests` + `csv.writer` → Java 17 + Spring Boot 3.2.5 + `RestTemplate`
- **Structure**: Model classes (`Game`, `Bookmaker`, `Market`, `Outcome`, `OddsRow`), services (`OddsFetcher`, `OddsParser`, `CsvWriter`), `CommandLineRunner` orchestrator
- **Docker**: Multi-stage build (Maven build → JRE 17 runtime)
- **Tests**: 14 JUnit 5 tests (13 unit + 1 benchmark)

## CSV optimization

Benchmarked 6 strategies at 200K rows × 20 iterations (3 runs). Winner: **BufferedWriter 64KB + row-level string concat**, 16–33% faster than all alternatives.

```
1) BufferedWriter 64KB + row concat    533 ms  ★ BEST
3) FileWriter + row concat             617 ms    +16%
5) In-memory StringBuilder             642 ms    +20%
6) BufferedOutputStream + getBytes     658 ms    +23%
2) BufferedWriter 64KB + direct writes 670 ms    +26%
4) PrintWriter + println               719 ms    +35%
```

**Why it wins**: 64KB buffer minimizes syscalls; Java's `invokedynamic` `StringConcatFactory` (the `+` operator since Java 9) produces an optimized single string per row → 1 `write()` call instead of 7, reducing `BufferedWriter`'s per-call synchronization overhead.

```java
// One write per row — leverages JVM-optimized string concat
writer.write(
    escapeCsv(row.getHomeTeam()) + ","
    + escapeCsv(row.getAwayTeam()) + ","
    + escapeCsv(row.getBookmaker()) + ","
    + escapeCsv(row.getOutcome()) + ","
    + row.getPrice() + "\n");
```

Full analysis with methodology and results in `docs/PERFORMANCE.md`.

body:
### Motivation

- Convert the README reference implementation from Python to Java to provide a Java-friendly sample implementation and clarify types and constants.
- Correct the reported character counts and character set to keep the total base (`BASE = 88`) consistent.

### Description

- Replaced Python code snippets with Java equivalents for mixed-radix encoding, `detail`/`n` arithmetic, and Base-88 conversions using typed variables and constants such as `BASES`, `CHARS`, and `BASE`.

title: ⚡ Bolt: implement arena allocator pattern to replace self-referential structs
body:
This PR adds a comprehensive implementation of the **Self-Referential Data Structures (Arena Allocator / Indices)** pattern, addressing a core paradigm shift needed when moving from Java/Python/C++ to Rust. It demonstrates how to effectively build graphs and complex structures using contiguous memory and stable indices, thereby sidestepping borrow checker errors and avoiding memory leaks associated with `Rc<RefCell<T>>` soups.

Additionally, this PR fixes a pre-existing failing test in `src/concurrency/thread_local.rs` which was blocking the `cargo test` suite from succeeding.

- Full port of all comparison logic in `com.xmlcompare` package: `CompareOptions`, `Difference`, `XmlCompare`, `Main`


title: Refactor AnsibleModule to Java implementation
body:
This PR moves the `AnsibleModule` implementation from Python (`ansible_bridge.py`) to Java (`PythonAnsibleModuleMock.java`). This refactoring improves the maintainability and performance of the module mock by utilizing Java's native capabilities for file system operations and hashing, while maintaining the necessary Python interface for Ansible module compatibility. Key changes include:
- A new `PythonAnsibleModuleMock` class in Java that implements parameter specification parsing, `run_command` delegation, and various hashing and file attribute methods.
- Injection of a `Factory` for this mock into the GraalPy context via `TaskExecutor`.
- Delegation of `AnsibleModule` logic in `ansible_bridge.py` to the Java-backed mock.
- Improved stability by using `_deep_convert` to normalize complex types before they cross the Java-Python boundary.

title: Refactor AnsibleModule bridge logic
body:
Refactored the `AnsibleModule` bridge logic by migrating state management and decision-making from the Python layer to the Java layer. This simplifies the Python implementation and leverages Java for core processing. Key changes include:
- Moving flag initialization and storage to Java.
- Implementing result preparation logic (`getExitArgs`, `getFailArgs`) in Java.
- Delegating `boolean` evaluation to Java's `Truthiness` utility.
- Reducing state maintenance in the Python script by managing file attributes in Java.
